places.description: US Army Intelligence Center & NETCOM headquarters. Origin of Army intelligence operations since 1877.
places.name: Fort Huachuca

places.description: Home of the 355th Wing, A-10 operations, and AMARG — the world's largest aircraft boneyard (4,400+ airframes).
places.name: Davis-Monthan AFB

places.description: Largest fighter pilot training base in the western world. F-35A Lightning II training wing.
places.name: Luke Air Force Base

places.description: US Army testing facility spanning 1,300+ square miles of desert. Weapons, munitions, and autonomous systems testing.
places.name: Yuma Proving Ground

places.description: March 13, 1997 mass sighting corridor from Prescott to Tucson. Thousands of witnesses, two distinct events.
places.name: Phoenix Lights Corridor

places.description: Busiest border sector for migrant encounters. 262 miles of international boundary.
places.name: Tucson Border Sector

places.description: Critical border corridor. Saw 300%+ increase in encounters 2021-2023.
places.name: Yuma Border Sector

places.description: Largest nuclear power plant in the US. Only nuclear plant not located near a natural body of water.
places.name: Palo Verde Nuclear Generating Station

places.description: 710-foot concrete arch dam on the Colorado River. Lake Powell — critical water storage for the Lower Basin.
places.name: Glen Canyon Dam

places.description: 336-mile canal system delivering Colorado River water to Phoenix and Tucson. Lifeline for 80% of AZ population.
places.name: Central Arizona Project

places.description: Arizona's primary commercial airport. 44+ million passengers annually.
places.name: Phoenix Sky Harbor

places.description: Alleged energy vortex sites. New Age significance intersecting with tribal sacred lands.
places.name: Sedona Vortex Sites

places.description: Lost Dutchman's Gold Mine legend. Apache sacred territory. Unexplained disappearances on record.
places.name: Superstition Mountains

places.description: 2.7 million acre military range. Largest USAF air-to-ground gunnery range. Restricted airspace.
places.name: Barry M. Goldwater Range

places.description: Reported NSA signals intelligence facility near Fort Huachuca. Limited public information available.
places.name: NSA San Xavier Annex
